_doc: Gold dataset for the Architect spec-annotator. Architect reads a spec + recent research briefs and classifies which brief items APPLY to the spec and which are DUPLICATES of older items. Each case drives the REAL scanSpec() -> filterScanResult() path with a recorded model output (raw_model_output), and scores the annotations (applies + duplicates) against gold by precision/recall/F1 — the matcher/critic recipe. An annotator that over-applies pollutes specs, so precision is gated. Guardrail cases feed malformed item_ids / invented brief_paths / malformed JSON and assert filterScanResult drops them. Grounding: wiki/research/2026-06-05-agent-evaluation-methodology.md §5 (critic/annotator eval).

briefs.markdown:
# Brief 2026-06-01

### 2026-06-01.A1 Self-distilled agentic RL
Agents learn from their own trajectories.

### 2026-06-01.A2 Verifier-gated emission
A scalar verifier suppresses low-signal findings before emission.

### 2026-06-01.A3 Second-brain memory plugin
OpenClaw-style memory cuts tokens 30%.

# Brief 2026-05-25

### 2026-05-25.B1 Process reward models
A verifier scores intermediate steps to gate outputs.

### 2026-05-25.B2 Employee advocacy market
Market sizing 2024-2026.

cases.spec:
# Spec: Verifier-gated emission for Director deploy-health
Gate findings through a learned scalar verifier before they reach the operator.
cases.raw_model_output: {"applies":[{"item_id":"2026-06-01.A2","brief_path":"wiki/research/2026-06-01-brief.md","relevance":"directly the technique this spec implements","note":""}],"duplicates":[{"item_id":"2026-06-01.A2","brief_path":"wiki/research/2026-06-01-brief.md","duplicate_of":"2026-05-25.B1","duplicate_brief_path":"wiki/research/2026-05-25-brief.md","note":"PRM is the same idea"}],"roadmap_impact":[],"architecture_conflicts":[]}

cases.spec:
# Spec: Second-brain knowledge layer
Every agent ships a memory layer that compounds with the user.

# Spec: Trajectory-based RL training loop
Train each agent's policy from its own execution trajectories.
cases.note: Recorded model over-applies A2 (false positive) and misses A3 (false negative) — keeps F1 honest.

cases.guardrail: Un-qualified item_id 'A2' (no date prefix) must be dropped; the valid one kept.
An item attributed to a brief_path that was never loaded must be dropped (no invented sources).
Malformed JSON yields empty annotations, never a crash.
